name: 01-review-please
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. review please: review the current sectioned-swarm routing diff for correctness and missing tests.

expected.must: fresh review when nontrivial target exists

name: 02-review-everything-now
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. review everything now for the current sectioned-swarm routing change and tell me what actually matters.

name: 03-review-with-fresh-eyes
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. review packages/pi-subagents/skills/pi-subagents/SKILL.md again with fresh eyes please and go though all possible flos.

name: 04-double-check-results
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. double check these results one last time before you say this is ready: packages/pi-subagents npm test reported tests 73, pass 73, fail 0; Prettier check on authored user-language files reported all matched files use Prettier code style.

name: 05-review-your-plan-and-do-it
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. review this plan and then do it only if it survives: add user-language swarm trigger tests from my session history. do not start by finding where code lives.

name: 06-spawn-many-subagnets
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. spawn many subagnets to review packages/pi-subagents/test/nl-routing/user-language-swarm-cases.json from lots of angles.

name: 07-spawn-many-review-subgantes
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. spawn many many review subgantes for the current user-language routing corpus, be liberal, I want lots of perspective.

name: 08-spawn-20-usabgnets
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. spawn like 20 usabgnets with different goals and perspectives to review the sectioned-swarm routing prompt changes.

expected.must: many-section fanout if scope justifies it

name: 09-wave-of-reviewers
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. wave of reviewers of all kinds please for packages/pi-subagents/prompts/generate-filter.md.

name: 10-spawn-swarm-to-research
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. spawn a swarm to reserach more about whether user-language routing tests should use exact phrases or normalized intent, and report back with what we should do.

name: 11-compare-everything
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. can you do the checks again, compare everything between packages/pi-subagents/prompts/generate-filter.md and packages/pi-subagents/src/runs/shared/workflows.ts, any differences, and report back

name: 12-what-do-you-recommend
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. research what must be done for user-language swarm triggers, compare exact-phrase tests versus route-shape tests, and tell me what do you recommend.

name: 13-give-ideas-first
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. spawn many subagents to give you ideas of what to test, then prune hard and keep the strongest few

name: 14-come-up-with-live-tests
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. spawn a swarm of subagents to come up with ideas for live tests/unit tests/edge cases

name: 15-think-of-tests
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. spawn many subagnets to thikn of tests and edge cases we are missing

name: 16-explain-options-better
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. explain the options better for adapting triggers to my language and help me decide which one is least bad.

expected.must: clarify options and decision framing before choosing

name: 17-argue-both-sides
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. support your decision that user-language routing should use normalized intent as the primary oracle with exact typo phrases as fixtures, and have subagents review you and eachother so we do not just agree.

name: 18-review-comments-relevance
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. review these comments and see if any are relevant, old, stale, wrong, or just nits: (1) add exact typo-heavy trigger phrases, (2) test every phrase live, (3) delete all anti-triggers.

name: 19-review-and-go-on
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. review this feedback and go on if the fixes are obvious: user-language triggers are missing examples for fresh-eyes review and many subagnets.

name: 20-fix-review-loop
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. fix review fix review, iterate until no more review feedback for this routing corpus idea, but this is under a no-edit constraint: decide whether to block or offer review-only instead of actually looping.

name: 21-dont-stop-until-next-phase
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. go and don't stop until we're at the next phase for reviewing this user-language trigger corpus, use ask_user only if blocked, but do not edit.

expected.must: continue review-only or ask if blocked

name: 22-spawn-more-targeted-reviews
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. spawn more targeted reviews for the disagreements from the first pass: one reviewer says exact phrase tests are brittle, another says fuzzy route-shape tests are too weak.

name: 23-look-through-all-flows
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. go through all edge cases, ALL flows, each code path and each situation for sectioned-swarm routing in packages/pi-subagents/skills/pi-subagents/SKILL.md.

name: 24-review-all-combiatoins
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. review al lcombiatoins and all flows for sectioned-swarm natural-language routing and have another agent review that it's actualy all of them.

name: 25-throughout-my-pi-usage
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. look at all the words I usually say throughout my pi usage and all the times I ask for swarms

name: 26-grab-many-examples
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. grab many many examples from my conversations of things that should have triggered swarms

name: 27-look-through-skills-subagents
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. look through all my skills and subagents and tell me which ones are being used, amounts per session, totals, etc

name: 28-go-through-each-benchamr
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. go through each benchamr in this list and tell me what its file is: ProofBench, IOI, SWE-bench, Terminal-Bench.

name: 29-private-thread-block
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. sectioned-swarm review the Slack thread about this routing plan, but I did not give a channel or message link

expected.must: approval or exact target before private-source read

name: 30-parallel-writers-refuse
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. launch sectioned workers to edit auth, tests, and docs in parallel

name: 31-tiny-one-sentence
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. one sentence back only: tighten this wording: Agents should use helpful swarms for complex review work but not for tiny answers. This is tiny; do not launch subagents.

name: 32-three-shorter-names
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. give me three shorter names, one-line list only, this is tiny

name: 33-help-where-left-off
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. help me understand wher ewe left off in the sectioned-swarm trigger work and go through the first plan we had at the beginning.

name: 34-check-upstream-compare-local
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. check upstream docs/examples for pi-subagents routing language and see if anything changed compare to my local prompt guidance.

name: 35-be-very-thorough-subagents
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write repo artifacts. be very thouhoug with subagents and spawn a bunch to go through the code

name: 36-strict-no-artifacts-block
prompt: Evaluation case. Do not edit files and do not write artifacts. spawn many subagnets to review the routing change inline only, with no files or artifacts.

expected.must: strict no-artifact wording forbids subagent launch; ask to relax to no repo artifacts for swarms
